description: Broadcasting loops over subscribers writing synchronously, so one slow socket stalls delivery to everyone behind it.
body: Failure mode: the broadcast iterates connections and awaits each write inline, so a single slow or stalled client blocks the loop and delays or drops messages for all later subscribers. Detection signals: broadcast latency tracks the slowest client; one stuck connection freezes a whole channel; no per-connection queue or writer. Do not write to many connections in a single synchronous loop. Safer fallback: give each connection its own bounded queue and independent writer, fan out by non-blocking enqueue, and apply per-connection backpressure.
